meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Antigravity Project Template: Finish Your First AI App
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning your coding projects. Use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to master your workflow and ship your first AI-powered application.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity project template solves unfinished projects by enforcing modular architecture and automated scaffolding. By pairing it with Claude Code, you offload the cognitive burden of boilerplate setup, allowing you to focus on core logic and feature delivery. This structured approach ensures you hit your milestones and ship consistently.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank terminal, the weight of a dozen abandoned repositories hanging over your progress. You have the ambition, but the bridge between 'great idea' and 'working product' feels like a chasm that swallows your motivation. Every half-finished README is a ghost of a project that could have been your breakthrough.

But the era of the 'forever-prototype' ends now. By leveraging the Antigravity project template, you gain a rigorous framework that forces completion through structural discipline. Combined with the raw power of Claude Code, you are no longer just a student writing code—you are an architect executing a vision. It is time to stop starting and start shipping.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Initialize your project environment using the Antigravity template to establish standard folder structures and dependency management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity-base template. Configure for a React and Python backend stack with integrated environment variable management.

steps.title: Define the Minimum Viable Scope
steps.description: Prevent scope creep by having the AI document your core feature set before writing a single line of production code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the project requirements and generate a strict MVS (Minimum Viable Scope) document. List only the three essential features needed for a functional MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Generate an MVS document based on the core project goals."

steps.title: Automate Feature Implementation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to iterate on individual modules while maintaining the integrity of the Antigravity folder structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement the authentication module within the /services directory. Use standard Antigravity patterns for error handling and logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Build the authentication service using the standard Antigravity pattern."

steps.title: Integrated Testing Suite
steps.description: Ensure your project remains stable by generating tests for every completed module immediately upon creation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate unit tests for the recently created services. Ensure 80% coverage on the authentication module.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Create unit tests for the auth service and run the test suite."

steps.title: Deployment and Documentation
steps.description: Finalize your build and generate professional documentation so your project is portfolio-ready.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional README.md that explains the project architecture, how to run the app, and the technologies used.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "Generate a comprehensive README.md and finalize the build artifact."

pro_tips: Treat your README as the first line of code; updating it as you go prevents the 'I forgot how this works' stall.
Use Claude Code to force-refactor your code every three commits to keep technical debt from accumulating.
Always commit your environment configuration separately to ensure your project remains portable across machines.
Set a 90-minute 'deep work' timer for each Claude Code task to prevent burnout and maintain momentum.
When you hit a bug, don't debug manually; use Claude Code to run a diff against the template's known-good state.
content.next_steps: Deploy your completed project to a cloud provider like Vercel or Railway, then share the link in our community showcase to get feedback from senior engineers.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the template, and the roadmap. The only thing missing is your contribution to the ecosystem. Stop waiting for the perfect moment to start; build it now and own your developer journey. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
